reasoning: ADCs are addressed in 3A001.a.5. For resolution of 16 bits or more, control applies only when the sample/output rate exceeds 0.18 GSPS (180 MSPS). This 24-bit converter operates at 256 kSPS (0.000256 GSPS), far below the threshold, so it is not described by 3A001 or any other CCL entry; it is subject to the EAR as EAR99.

item_name: Quad RF-DAC / quad RF-ADC mixed-signal front-end
reasoning: Integrated mixed-signal IC containing high-speed data converters described in 3A001.a.5. The four RF-DAC channels operate at 12 GSPS, far exceeding the 3A001.a.5.b digital-to-analog converter sample-rate thresholds (>3.5 GSPS for ≥10-bit parts), and the four 4 GSPS RF-ADC channels likewise exceed 3A001.a.5.a analog-to-digital converter limits (>1.2 GSPS). Such high-speed converters fall under Category 3 electronics, ECCN 3A001.a.5.

item_name: 6 GSPS RF digital-to-analog converter, to 5 GHz
reasoning: The item is a digital-to-analog converter. Under 3A001.a.6, DACs with a resolution of 12 bit or more and a 'sample rate' greater than 1,250 MSPS are controlled (3A001.a.6.b). This device has 16-bit resolution and a 6 GSPS (6,000 MSPS) update rate, exceeding both thresholds.

item_name: Wideband 2x2 RF agile transceiver, 47 MHz-6 GHz
reasoning: This is a wideband RF agile transceiver IC integrating data converters and frequency synthesizers with an LO tunable 47 MHz-6 GHz. Such microwave/RF electronic components with integrated frequency synthesizer assemblies operating into the GHz range fall under CCL Category 3 (Electronics), specifically 3A001.b covering microwave/RF electronic devices, with the integrated tunable frequency synthesizer aligning with 3A001.b.4. (Note: if specific synthesizer switching-time/ADC sample-rate thresholds are not exceeded, portions may default lower, but the component class is 3A001.)

A 300 MHz single-core 32-bit audio DSP for commercial audio mixing/filtering/equalization does not meet the performance thresholds or specialized criteria of any CCL Category 3 entry (e.g., 3A001 microprocessor/ADC/DAC controls). General-purpose commercial audio signal processing devices of this modest performance are not specifically controlled and fall under EAR99.

item_name: Narrow-band sub-1 GHz ISM RF transceiver
reasoning: This is a radio communications transceiver operating in sub-1 GHz ISM bands with FSK/GFSK modulation for wireless data links. As a telecommunications transmitting/receiving device it falls under CCL Category 5 Part 1. However, standard low-power ISM-band metering transceivers are typically mass-market commercial items. While 5A001 covers telecommunications equipment, a commodity-grade narrow-band ISM transceiver without frequency-hopping/spread-spectrum control features or encryption would most likely classify as EAR99; absent listed controlled features such as digital techniques exceeding thresholds, this metering RF transceiver is best treated as EAR99.

item_name: Integrated dual-channel RF transceiver, 75 MHz-6 GHz
reasoning: Item is a highly integrated RF transceiver IC for software-defined radio and wireless/telecom infrastructure operating 75 MHz-6 GHz with ~200 MHz bandwidth. It is telecommunications equipment/components subject to the EAR. Its parameters (tuning range, modest instantaneous bandwidth, on-chip synthesizers/filtering, and embedded ADC/DAC well below the sample-rate/resolution thresholds of 3A001.a.5 and lacking the specialized features of 5A001.b) do not rise to the level of the more restrictive 5A001 telecom controls, so it falls under the catch-all telecommunications entry 5A991.b.

Heterogeneous general-purpose microprocessor/microcomputer SoC (ARM Cortex + SHARC DSP cores) for commercial audio/industrial use; not meeting the specialized performance/radiation thresholds of 3A001, it falls under 3A991.a as a microprocessor/microcomputer microcircuit not controlled by 3A001.

This is an information security commodity employing cryptography with symmetric AES and asymmetric ECDSA/ECDH public-key algorithms for data confidentiality. It is described under Category 5 Part 2, 5A002.a, which covers items designed to use cryptography employing symmetric algorithms (>56-bit) or asymmetric algorithms based on discrete logarithms/elliptic curves. Hardware crypto accelerators with key storage and TRNG implementing these algorithms fall within 5A002.a.

item_name: 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi + Bluetooth module, 4 MB flash
reasoning: This is a wireless transceiver module providing 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi (802.11 b/g/n) and Bluetooth/BLE connectivity. Standard short-range commercial wireless radio equipment falls under Category 5 Part 2 telecommunications. Absent controlled cryptographic functionality exceeding mass-market thresholds, such consumer-grade wireless modules are classified under 5A992.c as items not controlled by 5A002 but performing information security functions / mass-market telecom equipment.

Field-programmable logic devices (FPGAs) are integrated circuits controlled under Category 3 (Electronics), ECCN 3A001.a.7 covers field programmable logic devices. While basic FPGAs of this scale/complexity may be low-cost commodity parts, FPGAs as a device class fall under 3A001.a.7 absent meeting specific decontrol thresholds; the general programmable logic device entry applies.

This secure element implements cryptography for data confidentiality (AES-128 symmetric algorithm) and asymmetric ECC (ECDSA/ECDH), making it 'information security' equipment described by Category 5 Part 2. Per 5A002.a, items designed or modified to perform cryptographic functions using symmetric algorithms with key length exceeding 56 bits or asymmetric algorithms based on elliptic curves over a finite field exceeding 112 bits are controlled. AES-128 and ECC exceed these thresholds, so the IC is classified 5A002.a.

This is a general-purpose 16-bit microcontroller/digital signal controller (60 MIPS) for motor control and embedded applications. Standard commercial MCUs/DSCs of this performance level are not described by any specific CCL entry under Category 3 (e.g., 3A001 microprocessors/DSPs do not capture ordinary embedded controllers at this performance). Absent specialized radiation-hardened or military-grade features, it is subject to the EAR but not listed, hence EAR99.

This is a standard Layer-2 managed Ethernet switching device for telecommunications/networking. It is not high-performance enough to meet 5A002 cryptographic controls or the higher performance thresholds of 5A001. As a network switching/communications apparatus, it falls under the catch-all telecommunications equipment entry 5A991.b.4 (switching equipment), controlled for Anti-Terrorism reasons.

A standard 10/100 Mbps Ethernet PHY transceiver compliant with IEEE 802.3 is a commercial, commodity networking component. It does not meet the performance thresholds or special characteristics of Category 5 Part 1 telecom controls (5A991), nor any other CCL entry, so it is subject to the EAR but classified EAR99.

A single-port 10/100 Mbps Ethernet PHY transceiver is a low-speed commodity networking component. It does not meet any performance thresholds in Category 5 Part 1 (telecommunications) such as high aggregate data rates, and provides no information security functions controlled under 5A002. As a basic commercial embedded networking IC, it is subject to the EAR but not described by any CCL entry, making it EAR99.

The MCU integrates a hardware cryptographic engine implementing AES (symmetric algorithm employing a key length exceeding 56 bits) along with 3DES, SHA, and an RNG. Items 'designed or modified to use cryptography employing digital techniques performing any cryptographic function other than authentication or digital signature' and supporting symmetric algorithms with key lengths >56 bits are controlled under Category 5 Part 2, ECCN 5A002.a.1. The crypto functionality is the controlling characteristic; the MCU's processing, memory, and CAN/USB/Ethernet interfaces are otherwise EAR99-level features.

This is a short-range Bluetooth 4.2 LE radio transceiver operating in the 2.4 GHz band for wireless connectivity. Such consumer/commercial wireless communications equipment with standard (non-strong) functionality falls under Category 5 Part 1 telecommunications. It is not a controlled high-end radio under 5A001, and as an information security/communications transceiver it is classified under 5A992.c as mass-market telecommunications equipment subject to the EAR but not requiring strong control.

item_name: Quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 compute module (2 GB / 16 GB)
reasoning: A general-purpose quad-core ARM Cortex-A72 SoM at 1.5 GHz with 2 GB RAM is a low-performance commodity computing module that does not meet the Adjusted Peak Performance or other thresholds of 4A003, nor any microprocessor parameters in 3A001. Its wireless connectivity uses standard commercial protocols without controlled cryptographic functionality rising to 5A002 (mass-market/standard). It is subject to the EAR but not described by a specific CCL entry, so it falls under EAR99.

A general-purpose 32-bit ARM Cortex-M4 microcontroller (168 MHz, 1MB flash, 192KB SRAM) is a commodity-grade embedded controller. It does not meet the technical thresholds of 3A001.a.7 (microprocessor microcircuits) or other Category 3 microelectronics controls, and lacks any specialized military, cryptographic, or performance characteristics that would place it on the CCL. Such mass-market MCUs are subject to the EAR but not described by any CCL entry, hence EAR99.

A standard broadband dielectric mirror for the visible band (400-750 nm) on fused silica does not meet the technical thresholds of 6A004 mirrors (e.g., specialized deformable optics, dimensional stability, or coatings for wavelengths >0.8 microns/high-power laser optics). General-purpose visible-band folding mirrors are not described by any CCL entry and are classified EAR99.

A kinematic mirror mount is a passive opto-mechanical positioning fixture (tip/tilt adjustment for a 1-inch optic). It is not an optical element itself and does not meet the controlled mount/positioning thresholds in Category 6 (e.g., 6A004 optical mirror/mount controls require specialized characteristics such as deformable/active mirrors or specific positioning performance). A standard manual two-screw kinematic mount has no controlled parameters and is therefore EAR99.

A single-core 32-bit ARM Cortex-A8 microprocessor running at 1.0 GHz is a commodity general-purpose embedded applications processor. It does not meet the performance thresholds or specialized characteristics of microprocessors controlled under Category 3 (e.g., 3A001.a.3 for adjusted peak performance, radiation hardening, or operation outside standard temperature ranges) or 4A003 high-performance computing aggregations. Such mass-market embedded MPUs for HMI/industrial use fall outside CCL entries and are classified EAR99.
